title: The Invoice Queue Retry
alert: Duplicate invoice work items are appearing after a retry.

briefing.objective: Trace why a retry can enqueue the same invoice work item more than once.
briefing.successCriterion: The Python unittest suite must show that each invoice appears only once after retries.
briefing.rootCause: The queue records every retry without checking whether that invoice is already pending.
briefing.evidence: The verification suite counts the queued invoice IDs after repeated enqueue attempts.

telemetry.impact: Retry traffic is creating duplicate invoice work and delaying reconciliation.

events.message: Invoice queue depth grew faster than successful processing.
The same invoice ID was accepted after a retry.
Reconciliation found repeated pending work for one invoice.

stakeholderMessages.author: Lena - Finance
stakeholderMessages.body: The settlement batch is waiting on reconciliation. We see one invoice represented by several pending work items, not several invoices.

stakeholderMessages.author: Maya - PM
stakeholderMessages.body: Reconciliation is delayed by duplicate invoice work. Can we contain retries before the next settlement run?

stakeholderMessages.author: Ash - On-call
stakeholderMessages.body: Queue depth grew after retry-worker acknowledgements timed out. The duplicate IDs are byte-for-byte identical.

stakeholderMessages.author: Jon - Senior Eng
stakeholderMessages.body: Before changing ordering, what invariant should remain true when the same invoice is retried?

stakeholderMessages.author: AI Pair
stakeholderMessages.body: I prepared three queue changes. Trace queue membership separately from display order before selecting one.
